item.title: Goodbye my love
item.description: My wife is now at peace. No more pain. After 1000+ lab results, 100+ operations and countless sleepless nights. And 7 years of fearing this Day. Now Im gonna look to the future and love my two daughters like there is no tomorrow. But there is, there is a tomorrow and its gonna be Ok.

item.dc:creator: ohvalleyofplenty

item.title: The Satanic Panic
item.description: Cat tax. My parents didn't even let me have the books in the 1st place. I had to invent my own system using the only dice I owned (a d12 and a d6) and disguise my GMing with friends in my room as listening to music. As a result our adventures were heavily influenced by The Cure (we went to school with that band and it was considered OK as they were 'nice young christian men') and Bauhaus (my parents couldn't tell the difference between their music) and Siouxsie and the Banshees (my parents figured if we were listening to a 'girl band' we were not up to 'sinful gay stuff'... jokes on them I turned out super-queer), with plotlines stolen from Dramarama, Into the Labyrinth, and Marmalade Atkins. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ For those interested in my home-brew rules having only been exposed to D&D through word-of-mouth: You had 12 points to spend between three 'stats', but you could pick what your stats were called. If you had a stat that was relevant to something you rolled a d12 and tried to get under or equal to your stat number, and if you rolled exactly on the stat something really cool happened. If you didn't have a relevant stat then you rolled a d6 and on an even you just about succeeded but on an odd something bad happened. Everything bad had 1d6+6 hit points, dragons had 2d12+12 hit points, and adventurers started each day with 1d6+12 hit points. Plate and/or magic armor subtracted 1d6 from the damage of enemy attacks, if you were wearing no armor it added 2 to the damage of enemy attacks. Adventurers and dragons did 1d12 damage, regular monsters did 1d6 damage. All attacks automatically hit. As far as I can recall we had no concept of things like experience points, levels, etc so character improvement was through finding magic weapons or learning spells. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Our adventures revolved around a travelling circus, and fighting dragons who were trying to build dungeons.
item.dc:creator: ThatOtherGirlYouKnow

item.dc:creator: BootsDusty

item.title: It's worse than this.
item.description: In 2020, Kroger bought back a billion dollars in shares. This is purchasing shares from investors, to prop up the share price. https://ir.kroger.com/CorporateProfile/press-releases/press-release/2021/Kroger-Board-of-Directors-Approves-1-Billion-Share-Repurchase-Authorization/default.aspx In 2021, they have instituted a similar program. This is approximately a dollar an hour raise for all employees, if it was instead used for wages. In addition, the other board members of Kroger share about another $20M. https://www1.salary.com/KROGER-CO-Executive-Salaries.html
item.dc:creator: SithElephant

item.title: Day 232: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me

Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.title: We support you!!
item.description: Pretty much offered them a slap in the face for 3 years as part of their "Here's a little bit more money but it's coming out of my profits so you should feel bad" Honesty it was pennies an hour. Corporations ~Do not care about you~ We need to MAKE THEM! https://nypost.com/2022/01/13/kroger-workers-strike-as-employees-cant-afford-groceries-report/
item.dc:creator: BernieMittens

item.title: Living in the US
item.dc:creator: soyamin

item.title: Worst day I've had in a long time
item.description:
Bf and I have known each other for a long time. I know there's two sides to every story, but I think we're at an impasse. We've been dating for 2 years, and friends for nearly a decade. Worked out we were both single and we tried it and it started off smoothly but the fights we had were weird. Looking back, they were all the same: something would go wrong, and then all his regrets would be showcased to me as if I was responsible. He's got an ex with a kid and the custody isn't dynamite but we git to see kid once a quarter for 3 weeks. I get how he wants to be there more for [name redacted], but his ex is tough to deal with on the best days. What I can't forgive him for is how he treats me and my pets when he's angry. He complains he has no stress relief but won't find comfort in anything or anyone. I know cats are difficult but I have good girls; he's so petrified they'll pee on anything that he wants me to keep them locked away for the rest of their lives. And then when I try to meet middle grounds about their living arrangements (keeping the cats out of our bedroom, etc) suddenly the fight's about how I'm too passive and don't love him and that I should have done some thing when he stormed away from me. In his good moods, he's perfect for me, but I can't keep him here if he wants to leave. I'm just not built to try and convince someone to stay with me. Guess this is the start to a new chapter in my life.
Calypso
Juliette
